[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom - allowance not decremented on max uint256] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim calls approve(attacker, type(uint256).max); attacker calls transferFrom multiple times) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim approves type(uint256).max; 2. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount1); 3. _approve decrements: type(uint256).max - amount1 which may still be type(uint256).max due to SafeMath; 4. attacker calls transferFrom again for amount2), violating INVARIANT (transferFrom must always decrement allowance by the transferred amount; if allowance is type(uint256).max it should still be decremented), causing scoped impact: attacker can drain victim's entire USDS balance in multiple transactions without allowance being consumed, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: unit test with approve(attacker, type(uint256).max), call transferFrom twice, assert allowance decrements correctly each time and total transferred does not exceed balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: sendToPool - balance vs accounting mismatch] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (sendToPool moves USDS from _sender to _poolAddress without checking StabilityPool's totalUSDSDeposits accounting; StabilityPool._sendUSDStoStabilityPool increments totalUSDSDeposits after sendToPool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. if a reentrancy path exists where sendToPool is called but totalUSDSDeposits is not incremented; 2. attacker deposits USDS that is moved to StabilityPool but not counted in totalUSDSDeposits), violating INVARIANT (StabilityPool.totalUSDSDeposits must always equal USDSToken._balances[stabilityPoolAddress]; any USDS moved to StabilityPool via sendToPool must be reflected in totalUSDSDeposits), causing scoped impact: accounting mismatch allows attacker to withdraw more USDS than deposited, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting usdsToken.balanceOf(stabilityPool) == stabilityPool.getTotalUSDSDeposits() after every provideToSP/withdrawFromSP sequence.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transfer to SableStakingV2] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (_requireValidRecipient does not block SableStakingV2 address; SableStakingV2 holds USDS as fee gains tracked by F_USDS accumulator) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transfer(sableStakingAddress, amount) sending USDS directly to SableStakingV2; 2. SableStakingV2's USDS balance increases but F_USDS is not updated), violating INVARIANT (SableStakingV2's USDS token balance must equal the sum of all staker entitlements tracked by F_USDS; directly transferred USDS must not create unaccounted surplus claimable by stakers), causing scoped impact: USDS sent directly to SableStakingV2 creates an unaccounted surplus that existing stakers can claim as excess USDS rewards beyond their entitlement? Local proof idea: unit test calling transfer(sableStakingAddress, 1e18), then check if any staker can withdraw more USDS than their F_USDS-based entitlement.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve race - victim uses decreaseAllowance as mitigation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim tries to safely reduce allowance by calling decreaseAllowance(attacker, delta) instead of approve; attacker monitors mempool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim calls decreaseAllowance(attacker, delta) to reduce allowance from X to X-delta; 2. attacker front-runs by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, X) draining full current allowance; 3. victim's decreaseAllowance reverts because allowance is now 0 and subtraction underflows), violating INVARIANT (decreaseAllowance must be a safe alternative to approve for reducing allowance; it must not create a window where attacker can drain the full original allowance), causing scoped impact: attacker drains full original allowance X before the decrease takes effect, gaining excess USDS? Local proof idea: unit test with initial allowance 100e18, attacker drains 100e18, victim's decreaseAllowance(50e18) reverts, assert attacker received 100e18.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit + approve race interaction] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim has used permit to set allowance for attacker; victim then calls approve(attacker, 0) to revoke; attacker monitors mempool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. permit sets allowance[victim][attacker] = X; 2. victim calls approve(attacker, 0) to revoke; 3. attacker front-runs approve(attacker, 0) by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, X); 4. victim's approve(attacker, 0) lands setting allowance to 0), violating INVARIANT (a victim's attempt to revoke allowance via approve(spender, 0) must not be front-runnable to drain the existing permit-set allowance), causing scoped impact: attacker drains X USDS before revocation, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: unit test simulating front-run of approve(0) by transferFrom, assert attacker received X and allowance is now 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transfer / transferFrom - GasPool USDS lock] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (GasPool holds USDS gas compensation; _requireValidRecipient does not block GasPool; attacker can transfer USDS to GasPool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transfer(gasPoolAddress, amount) sending extra USDS to GasPool; 2. GasPool now holds more USDS than the single gas compensation unit; 3. TroveManager's _sendGasCompensation calls returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, liquidator, gasCompensation) which can only send the recorded gas compensation amount), violating INVARIANT (GasPool USDS balance must equal exactly one gas compensation unit per active trove; excess USDS in GasPool must not be claimable by liquidators as extra compensation), causing scoped impact: if liquidator can claim excess GasPool USDS via returnFromPool, they receive more than the entitled gas compensation? Local proof idea: unit test sending extra USDS to GasPool, then simulate liquidation gas compensation claim, assert liquidator receives only the entitled amount.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom - victim is a contract with receive hook] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim is a smart contract that has a receive() or fallback() function; attacker has allowance on victim; transferFrom sends USDS to attacker who is a contract) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attackerContract, amount); 2. _transfer executes, moving USDS to attackerContract; 3. attackerContract's receive() is NOT triggered since USDS transfer is ERC20 not ETH; 4. no reentrancy via ERC20 transfer since no callback), violating INVARIANT (ERC20 transfers must not trigger reentrancy; _balances must be consistent before and after transfer), causing scoped impact: confirm no reentrancy path exists through ERC20 transfer in USDSToken? Local proof idea: unit test with reentrant attacker contract attempting to call transferFrom again inside a hook, assert no double-spend occurs.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit - owner == spender self-permit] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (permit allows owner == spender; attacker signs a permit where owner = attacker and spender = attacker) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls permit(attacker, attacker, type(uint256).max, maxDeadline, v, r, s); 2. _allowances[attacker][attacker] = type(uint256).max; 3. attacker calls transferFrom(attacker, recipient, amount)), violating INVARIANT (self-permit must not grant any capability beyond what transfer() already provides; _allowances[owner][owner] must not enable any additional value extraction), causing scoped impact: self-permit with max allowance allows attacker to use transferFrom path instead of transfer, potentially bypassing any future transfer restrictions while gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: unit test with self-permit, assert transferFrom(self, recipient, amount) behaves identically to transfer(recipient, amount) with no additional capability.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve race - multi-step reward extraction via StabilityPool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim has USDS and is a StabilityPool depositor with accumulated BNB/SABLE gains; victim calls withdrawFromSP(0) to claim gains without withdrawing principal; attacker has allowance on victim) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim calls withdrawFromSP(0) which pays out SABLE/BNB gains and resets snapshots; 2. victim receives SABLE tokens; 3. attacker front-runs victim's subsequent provideToSP by draining victim's USDS via transferFrom; 4. victim cannot re-deposit, losing future reward eligibility), violating INVARIANT (a depositor's ability to re-enter the StabilityPool after claiming gains must not be preventable by an approved spender; reward eligibility must not be manipulable via allowance exploitation), causing scoped impact: attacker prevents victim from re-depositing after gain claim, attacker deposits instead and captures future SABLE/BNB rewards? Local proof idea: integration test simulating withdrawFromSP(0) followed by attacker draining USDS, assert victim cannot provideToSP.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transferFrom - allowance deduction after transfer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (transferFrom calls _transfer then _approve for allowance deduction; if _transfer reverts, allowance is not consumed) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, recipient, amount) where amount > victim's balance; 2. _transfer reverts due to SafeMath underflow; 3. allowance is not decremented), violating INVARIANT (a failed transferFrom must not consume allowance; allowance must only be decremented on successful transfers), causing scoped impact: confirm that failed transfers do not consume allowance, and that this cannot be exploited to preserve allowance while appearing to transfer? Local proof idea: unit test calling transferFrom with amount > balance, assert revert and assert allowance is unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom - front-running victim's closeTrove repayment] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim needs to repay USDS debt to close their trove; victim has USDS balance >= debt; attacker has allowance on victim; BorrowerOperations.closeTrove calls burn(victim, debt)) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim calls closeTrove; 2. attacker front-runs by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, victim_balance) draining victim's USDS; 3. victim's closeTrove fails because burn(victim, debt) reverts due to insufficient balance), violating INVARIANT (a borrower's ability to close their trove must not be preventable by an approved spender draining their USDS; trove closure must not be blocked by allowance exploitation), causing scoped impact: victim cannot close trove, remains exposed to liquidation risk; attacker gains victim's USDS and victim loses collateral access? Local proof idea: integration test where attacker drains victim's USDS before closeTrove, assert closeTrove reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit + transferFrom - front-running victim's redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim holds USDS and intends to call TroveManager.redeemCollateral to exchange USDS for BNB; attacker has a valid permit signature from victim) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls permit(victim, attacker, amount, deadline, v, r, s); 2. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount) draining victim's USDS; 3. victim's redeemCollateral fails because they lack sufficient USDS), violating INVARIANT (a USDS holder's ability to redeem collateral must not be preventable by permit/allowance exploitation; redemption value must not be extractable by an attacker who drains the redeemer's USDS), causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's USDS and redeems it themselves, gaining BNB collateral that victim intended to receive? Local proof idea: integration test where attacker drains victim's USDS via permit+transferFrom before redeemCollateral, assert victim's redemption fails and attacker redeems instead.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _requireValidRecipient - incomplete blacklist] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (_requireValidRecipient blocks only stabilityPoolAddress, troveManagerAddress, borrowerOperationsAddress; CollSurplusPool is not blocked) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transfer(collSurplusPoolAddress, amount) sending USDS to CollSurplusPool; 2. CollSurplusPool has no mechanism to account for or return directly-transferred USDS), violating INVARIANT (USDS must not be transferable to CollSurplusPool which has no USDS accounting; any USDS sent to CollSurplusPool is permanently locked), causing scoped impact: USDS is permanently locked in CollSurplusPool, reducing circulating supply and distorting StabilityPool reward calculations? Local proof idea: unit test calling transfer(collSurplusPoolAddress, 1e18), assert USDS is locked and no recovery mechanism exists.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom - fuzz invariant: balance conservation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (arbitrary sequence of transfer, transferFrom, approve, permit, mint, burn operations) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (fuzz: any combination of USDSToken state-changing functions with arbitrary parameters), violating INVARIANT (sum of all _balances must always equal _totalSupply; no operation must create USDS from nothing or destroy it without corresponding totalSupply adjustment; _balances[address] must never be negative), causing scoped impact: balance/supply mismatch allows attacker to claim more USDS than exists, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: Foundry invariant test with handlers for all USDSToken functions, assert _totalSupply == sum of all tracked balances after every call.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit - fuzz nonce monotonicity] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (arbitrary sequence of permit calls with varying nonces) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (fuzz: permit calls with nonce values 0, 1, 2, ... and out-of-order nonces), violating INVARIANT (_nonces[owner] must be strictly monotonically increasing; a permit with nonce N must only succeed when _nonces[owner] == N; out-of-order nonces must revert), causing scoped impact: if nonce validation is weak, attacker replays old permit signatures to reset allowances and drain USDS repeatedly? Local proof idea: fuzz test with random nonce values in permit calls, assert only the call with nonce == _nonces[owner] succeeds and all others revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom - StabilityPool deposit share manipulation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (StabilityPool has multiple depositors; attacker has allowance on victim depositor; SABLE issuance is time-based) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker waits for a large SABLE issuance to accumulate; 2. attacker drains victim's USDS via transferFrom before victim can top up their deposit; 3. victim's compounded deposit shrinks relative to total; 4. attacker deposits the drained USDS to increase their own share; 5. attacker calls withdrawFromSP to claim disproportionate SABLE gains), violating INVARIANT (SABLE gains must be proportional to deposit share at the time of issuance; an attacker must not be able to increase their reward share by draining a competing depositor's USDS and re-depositing it), causing scoped impact: attacker captures SABLE rewards proportional to victim's deposit share, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: integration test with two depositors, attacker drains victim's USDS and re-deposits, assert attacker's SABLE gain exceeds their fair share.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: sendToPool - StabilityPool balance vs totalUSDSDeposits invariant] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (sendToPool is called by StabilityPool during provideToSP; totalUSDSDeposits is incremented after sendToPool; if sendToPool succeeds but totalUSDSDeposits increment fails due to overflow or reentrancy) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker crafts a provideToSP call with amount near type(uint256).max; 2. sendToPool moves USDS; 3. totalUSDSDeposits.add(_amount) overflows - but SafeMath prevents this; REVISED: test that SafeMath correctly prevents overflow), violating INVARIANT (totalUSDSDeposits must never overflow; usdsToken.balanceOf(stabilityPool) must always equal totalUSDSDeposits), causing scoped impact: if overflow were possible, totalUSDSDeposits would wrap to a small value, allowing attacker to withdraw more USDS than deposited? Local proof idea: fuzz test with large _amount values in provideToSP, assert SafeMath reverts on overflow and totalUSDSDeposits == usdsToken.balanceOf(stabilityPool).

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom - victim's USDS drained before repayUSDS in adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim calls adjustTrove to repay USDS debt; BorrowerOperations calls burn(victim, repayAmount); attacker has allowance on victim) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker front-runs victim's adjustTrove by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, victim_balance); 2. victim's adjustTrove fails because burn(victim, repayAmount) reverts due to insufficient balance), violating INVARIANT (a borrower's ability to repay debt and adjust their trove must not be preventable by an approved spender; debt repayment must not be blockable via allowance exploitation), causing scoped impact: victim cannot repay debt, trove becomes undercollateralized and subject to liquidation; attacker gains victim's USDS and victim loses collateral? Local proof idea: integration test where attacker drains victim's USDS before adjustTrove repayment, assert adjustTrove reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: returnFromPool - cross-module: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (TroveManager calls returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, liquidator, gasCompensation) in _sendGasCompensation; liquidator address is msg.sender of the liquidation call; attacker is the liquidator) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls liquidate(trove); 2. TroveManager calls returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, attacker, gasCompensation); 3. USDSToken._transfer(gasPoolAddress, attacker, gasCompensation) executes), violating INVARIANT (gas compensation must equal exactly the USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION constant; returnFromPool must not transfer more than the recorded gas compensation; GasPool balance must decrease by exactly gasCompensation), causing scoped impact: if gasCompensation calculation is inflated or GasPool has excess USDS from direct transfers, attacker receives more USDS than entitled as gas compensation? Local proof idea: unit test with extra USDS in GasPool, simulate liquidation, assert liquidator receives only USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION amount.
